column2:
So, I saw 'Thunderbolts,' and honestly, it felt a bit more fresh and grounded than some of the recent Marvel movies. The characters were pretty cool – you actually got to know them, though some definitely stood out more than others. It even had kind of a modern, almost political vibe to it, which was interesting.

The movie moved along at a good pace, didn't feel slow or anything. And yeah, even though it touched on some darker feelings and stuff, there was plenty of humor to keep it from getting too heavy. The final fight wasn't maybe the biggest spectacle ever, which was a little less than expected, but it was still thrilling in a different way, more about what the characters were going through internally, you know?

So, 'Mission: Impossible - Final Reckoning' – for me, it's just a notch below Fallout and Dead Reckoning, but seriously, it's still a really good movie! You don't get as many of the typical car chases or huge explosions, but man, the tension is real. You're constantly wondering if the characters are gonna make it or pull off whatever insane thing they're trying to do. It also does a good job of filling in some of the backstory, which was cool, and the story moves at a good clip.

So, I finally saw 'How to Train Your Dragon,' and since I hadn't seen the animated movies, it was all totally new to me. And wow, the story and characters were just so well developed – I felt completely pulled into their world right away! The visual effects looked really good too. The ending was epic, especially that whole third act, and I felt so many emotions watching it. Definitely a great movie overall!

So, I saw '28 Years Later,' and honestly, the story just felt kind of lacking for me. It was like they were more focused on setting up a sequel than making this movie feel complete. The whole storyline they tried to pay off at the end? It just wasn't set up well at all. I guess now I get how some people feel about certain Marvel movies, where it's all about the next one instead of the current one.

I checked out 'Jurassic World: Rebirth,' and for me, it was kind of a 5 out of 10. The script felt pretty weak right from the start, like they were rushing just to get everyone onto the island with the dinosaurs. Because of that, the characters felt super underdeveloped, and even the mercenary types didn't really act like proper mercenaries, which was weird. It did have some good action, but honestly, I missed seeing more of the non-violent dinosaurs – it was mostly just chaos. It just felt a bit rushed overall

Alright, so I'd give 'Superman' an 8 out of 10. They really jump straight into the story, which feels a little jarring at first, but honestly, they do a great job of catching you up as the movie goes on. What I really liked is how they made Superman feel vulnerable and not totally overpowered. It gets pretty sci-fi heavy in parts and just feels super true to a comic book, really pushing that 'beyond reality' believability, which is exactly what you want with superheroes. Plus, it had some good spots of humor, plenty of action, and a really nice reveal at the end!
